zh.lifeSummary: 當Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves於1334年出生在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福可列福時，他的父親Adolf II. von der Mark20歲，母親Margarete von Kleve24歲。他於1369年與Margarete von Jülich在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福可列福結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1381年被列為graf adolf i von kleve。他去世於1394年9月7日在他的家鄉享年60歲，埋葬在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福可列福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves于1334年出生在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福可列福时，他的父亲Adolf II. von der Mark20岁，母亲Margarete von Kleve24岁。他于1369年与Margarete von Jülich在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福可列福结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1381年被列为graf adolf i von kleve。他去世于1394年9月7日在他的家乡享年60岁，埋葬在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可列福可列福。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Mark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Dutch, and Sorbian: from Latin Marcus, the personal name of Saint Mark the Evangelist, author of the second Gospel in the New Testament. This name was also borne by a number of early Christian saints. Marcus is an ancient Roman name, of uncertain (possibly non-Italic) etymology; it may have some connection with the name of the war god Mars; compare Martin . The personal name was not as popular in England in the Middle Ages as it was elsewhere in Europe, especially in Italy, where the evangelist became the patron of Venice and the Venetian Republic. He was allegedly buried at Aquileia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Polish, Czech, and Slovak Marek , Ukrainian, Slovenian, Sorbian, Rusyn, and Croatian Marko , Greek Markos , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Jewish and Slavic Markowicz and Markovich ; see also 6 below.

English and German; Dutch (Van der Mark): topographic name for someone who lived on a boundary between two districts, from Middle English merke, Middle High German marc, Middle Dutch marke, merke, all meaning ‘borderland’. The German term also denotes an area of fenced-off land (see Marker 5) and, like the English word, is embodied in various placenames which have given rise to habitational names. This surname (in any of the possible senses; see also 5 below) is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine; compare Marck ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Marck, Pas-de-Calais, France.

variants.name: Marc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Andre, Aurele, Alexandre, Edeline, Lucien, Lucner, Mireille, Pierrot, Renald, Rochenel.
French, Haitian, and Romanian: from the personal name Marc (see Mark ).
Breton (mainly Finistère; also Le Marc): from the personal name Marc'h, from Breton marc'h ‘horse’, and probably also from the (French) personal name Marc, which is of Latin origin (see 1 and Mark ).

variants.name: Marcelin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Andre, Pierre, Mireille, Anselme, Calixte, Constant, Dominique, Elysee, Ernst, Francois, Fritz, Georges, Jean Claude, Jean-Claude.  Spanish Luis, Carlos, Enrique, Marcial, Mario, Raul, Rolando.

French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from the French personal name Marcel(l)in, from the Latin name Marcellinus, a double derivative of Marcus (see Mark ) borne by several early Christian saints, including a pope who died in the persecutions instigated by Diocletian. This surname is by far most common in Haiti.
Galician (Marcelín): habitational name from Marcelín, a village in Galicia, northern Spain.

variants.name: March
meanings: English: habitational name from March (Cambridgeshire) or from any of numerous minor English placenames deriving from Middle English marche ‘boundary’ (partly from Old English mearc in the dative-case form mearce, partly from Old French marche). Compare Mark 2.
Catalan: from the personal name March, Catalan equivalent of Mark 1. This surname is also found in southern France.
German: topographic name from Middle Low German march, Middle High German marc(h) ‘mark, sign, boundary’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word (Bavaria).

variants.name: Marcil
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Yvon, Alain, Alphonse, Amie, Andre, Hilaire, Jacques, Jean Charles, Jean Jacques, Jean-Jacques, Leandre, Normand.

French: from the personal name Marcil, from Latin Marcilius, a derivative of Marcius and this one of Marcus (see Mark ). This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany. Compare Marcell , Marcille , and Mercil .

variants.name: Martin
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Emilia, Jose, Berta, Consuelo, Eduardo, Francisco, Jose Tomas, Leopoldo, Margarita, Mario, Miguel Angel, Raul.

Italian and Hispanic (mainly Honduras): from the female personal name Marcia, feminine form of Latin Marcius, a derivative of Marcus (see Mark ). Marcia was the name of several early Christian martyrs and saints.

variants.name: Marck
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Brunel.
Flemish, Dutch, and German: variant of   Mark  .
French (Alsace and Lorraine): variant of   Marc   ‘Mark’, or a surname of German origin (see 1 above, compare   Mark   2). This surname is also found in Haiti, where it is rare.

variants.name: Marcel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Benoit, Pierre, Alphonse, Andre, Angelle, Antoine, Gislaine, Henri, Raymonde, Yanick, Yvanne.

French: from the personal name Marcel, from Latin Marcellus, a diminutive of Marcus (see Mark 1), which was borne by a 3rd-century Christian saint, a missionary to Gaul who was martyred at Bourges with his companion Anastasius (see also Marcello ).

variants.name: Marcello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Vito, Rocco, Sal, Antonio, Berardino, Carmine, Carmino, Domenico, Francesco, Gino, Giovanna.

Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Marcello, Latin Marcellus, a diminutive of Marcus (see Mark 1). This was borne by several early Christian saints, and consequently became popular as a personal name during the Middle Ages (see also Marcel ).

variants.name: Marciano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Salvatore, Carmine, Giovanni, Santo, Angelo, Antonio, Benedetto, Domenic, Francesco, Gaetano, Giacomo.
Italian (southern):
habitational name from any of various places called Marciano.

nameMeanings.name: Adolph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual (hypercorrected) spelling of German Adolf , which was first introduced into Britain by the Normans, displacing the Old English equivalent Æthelwulf (‘noble’ + ‘wolf ’). It did not become at all common, however, until it was reintroduced by the Hanoverians in the 18th century. The association with Adolf Hitler ( 1889–1945 ) has meant that the name has been little used since the Second World War. The Latinized form Adolphus has been a recurring name in the Swedish royal family and has also been used occasionally in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Adolphus
meanings: Usual (hypercorrected) spelling of German Adolf , which was first introduced into Britain by the Normans, displacing the Old English equivalent Æthelwulf (‘noble’ + ‘wolf ’). It did not become at all common, however, until it was reintroduced by the Hanoverians in the 18th century. The association with Adolf Hitler ( 1889–1945 ) has meant that the name has been little used since the Second World War. The Latinized form Adolphus has been a recurring name in the Swedish royal family and has also been used occasionally in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves was born in 1334, in Cleves, Kleve, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, his father, Adolf II. von der Mark, was 20 and his mother, Margarete von Kleve, was 24. He married Margarete von Jülich in 1369, in Cleves, Kleve, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. In 1381, at the age of 47, his occupation is listed as graf adolf i von kleve. He died on 7 September 1394, in his hometown, at the age of 60, and was buried in Cleves, Kleve, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves est né en 1334, à Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne, son père, Adolf II. von der Mark, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Margarete von Kleve, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Margarete von Jülich en 1369, à Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. En 1381, à l’âge de 47 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est graf adolf i von kleve. Il est décédé le 7 septembre 1394, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves 1334 in Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adolf II. von der Mark 20 und seine Mutter Margarete von Kleve 24. Er hat 1369 in Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland Margarete von Jülich geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1381, als er 47 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als graf adolf i von kleve aufgeführt. Er starb am 7. September 1394 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves è nato il nel 1334 nel Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania, il suo padre, Adolf II. von der Mark, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Margarete von Kleve, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Margarete von Jülich nel 1369, nel Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1381, all'età di 47 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come graf adolf i von kleve. Morì il 7 settembre 1394, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Clevesが1334年にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレブクレブで生まれた時，父Adolf II. von der Markは20歳，母Margarete von Kleveは24歳であった。1369年にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレブクレブでMargarete von Jülichと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1381年，47歳の時，職業はgraf adolf i von kleveと記録されている。彼は1394年9月7日に故郷で60歳で死去し，ドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレブクレブにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves(이)가 1334년 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 클브 클브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adolf II. von der Mark(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Margarete von Kleve(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1369년 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 클브 클브에서 Margarete von Jülich(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1381년, 47세의 나이로 그의 직업은 graf adolf i von kleve이었다. 그는 1394년 9월 7일 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 클브 클브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves nasceu em 1334, em Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha, seu pai, Adolf II. von der Mark, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margarete von Kleve, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Margarete von Jülich em 1369, em Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1381, com 47 anos, sua profissão aparece como graf adolf i von kleve. Ele faleceu em 7 de setembro de 1394, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves родился в 1334 г. в местности клив, клив, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия, его отцу по имени Adolf II. von der Mark было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margarete von Kleve, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Margarete von Jülich в 1369 г. в местности клив, клив, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. В 1381 году, в возрасте 47 лет,  он работал по профессии graf adolf i von kleve. Он умер 7 сентября 1394 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности клив, клив, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adolph III von der Mark Earl of Cleves nació en 1334, en Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania, su papá, Adolf II. von der Mark, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Margarete von Kleve, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Margarete von Jülich en 1369, en Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1381, a la edad de 47 años, su ocupación registrada era graf adolf i von kleve. Murió el 7 de septiembre de 1394, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Kleve, Kleve, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania.
